●Live Updates
◆🔴 BNS 2023 — Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita fully effective from July 1, 2024 (Replaces IPC 1860)◆⚡ BNSS 2023 — Zero FIR provision now mandatory across all states◆🔵 Supreme Court: Right to Privacy is a Fundamental Right under Article 21◆📋 BSA 2023 — Electronic evidence admissible without physical certification◆🏠 RERA Amendment — Builder penalty doubled, homebuyers rights strengthened◆⚖️ Motor Vehicles Act — Hit-and-run compensation increased to ₹2 Lakh◆📜 RTI Act — Response time reduced to 15 days for life/liberty matters◆🛡️ Companies Act Amendment 2024 — New CSR compliance norms effective Q1 2025◆👨‍⚖️ Fast Track Courts — POCSO cases must conclude within 6 months◆🔔 Consumer Protection — E-commerce return policy rules strengthened 2024

Know Your Rights
AI AssistantExplore Laws
Citizen Empowerment

Know Your Rights

A comprehensive guide to your legal rights as an Indian citizen. Know them. Assert them. Protect them.

⚖️

Fundamental Rights (Part III)

Right to Equality (Articles 14–18)

Right to Freedom (Article 19) — Speech, Assembly, Movement, Profession

Right Against Exploitation (Articles 23–24)

Right to Freedom of Religion (Articles 25–28)

Cultural and Educational Rights (Articles 29–30)

Right to Constitutional Remedies (Article 32)

Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Article 21)

Right to Education (Article 21A)

Right to Privacy (Puttaswamy Judgment 2017)

🛡️

Rights During Arrest (BNSS 2023)

Right to know grounds of arrest (Section 47 BNSS)

Right to inform a family member or friend (Section 50 BNSS)

Right to consult and be defended by a lawyer

Right to be produced before magistrate within 24 hours

Right to bail in bailable offences

Right to medical examination

Right to remain silent (cannot be forced to self-incriminate)

Right to free legal aid if unable to afford a lawyer

🛒

Consumer Rights (Consumer Protection Act 2019)

Right to Safety — protection from hazardous goods and services

Right to Information — about quality, quantity, price of goods

Right to Choose — access to variety at competitive prices

Right to be Heard — consumer interests considered in policy

Right to Redressal — seek remedy against unfair trade practices

Right to Consumer Education — awareness of rights

Right to file complaint in District/State/National Consumer Forum

E-commerce consumer protections under 2020 Rules

💼

Labour Rights

Right to minimum wage (Minimum Wages Act 1948)

Right to equal pay for equal work

Right to Provident Fund (EPF Act 1952)

Right to Gratuity after 5 years of service

Right to Maternity Benefit (26 weeks paid leave)

Right to safe working conditions

Right to form trade unions

Protection against wrongful termination

Right to compensation for workplace accidents

👩

Women's Rights

Protection from Domestic Violence (PWDVA 2005)

Protection from Dowry Harassment (Section 85 BNS)

Protection from Sexual Harassment at Workplace (POSH Act 2013)

Right to Maternity Benefit (26 weeks paid leave)

Equal pay for equal work

Right to maintenance after divorce

Protection under POCSO Act for girl children

Right to free legal aid in criminal cases

📋

Right to Information (RTI Act 2005)

Right to request information from any public authority

Information must be provided within 30 days

15 days for matters involving life or liberty

Fee: ₹10 for application, ₹2 per page for copies

BPL applicants are exempt from fees

First appeal to the same authority within 30 days

Second appeal to Information Commission within 90 days

Penalty of ₹250/day (max ₹25,000) for non-compliance

Need help asserting your rights?

Get Free Legal Help Read the Laws
